mcqs.question: Which of the following relations on the set {1, 2, 3} is reflexive?
mcqs.explanation: A reflexive relation must contain all pairs (a,a) for every element in the set. Only option A has (1,1), (2,2), and (3,3).

mcqs.question: Which property is exhibited by the relation 'is a sibling of' on a set of people?
mcqs.explanation: The 'is a sibling of' relation is symmetric because if A is a sibling of B, then B is a sibling of A. It is not reflexive (one cannot be their own sibling), not necessarily transitive (siblings of siblings might not be siblings), and not antisymmetric.

mcqs.question: A relation R on set A is antisymmetric if:

options.text: ∀a,b ∈ A, if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,a) ∈ R then a = b
∀a,b,c ∈ A, if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,c) ∈ R then (a,c) ∈ R

mcqs.explanation: The formal definition of antisymmetry is: ∀a,b ∈ A, if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,a) ∈ R then a = b. Option A defines symmetry, option C defines transitivity, and option D defines reflexivity.

mcqs.question: Consider the relation R = {(a,b), (b,c), (a,c)} on set {a,b,c}. Which properties does this relation have?
mcqs.explanation: The relation is transitive because whenever (a,b) and (b,c) are present, (a,c) is also present. It is antisymmetric because there are no symmetric pairs with different elements (no (b,a), (c,b), or (c,a)). It is not reflexive (missing (a,a), (b,b), (c,c)) and not symmetric (missing symmetric pairs).

mcqs.question: Which of the following is both symmetric and antisymmetric?

options.text: The relation 'is a subset of' on power sets
The equality relation '=' on any set

mcqs.explanation: The equality relation is both symmetric (if a = b then b = a) and antisymmetric (if a = b and b = a then a = b, which is always true when a and b are equal). The other relations are antisymmetric but not symmetric.
